ADJ.etymology: From eiginn, from *aiganaz, past participle of *aiganą. Equivalent to -en. Cognate with own.
From viss, from *gawissaz. Cognate with viss.

From enkil, from enkel, from *ainakulaz, equivalent to en + -el. Compare etymology of simple. Cognate with enkel, enkelt, enkel, ekkil, enkel and 𐌰𐌹𐌽𐌰𐌺𐌻𐍃. Compare also singulus with the same suffix. See also änka and änk
From tíðligr and tidelik, related to tid.
From dumber, from dumbr, from *dumbaz, from *dʰewbʰ-. Compare dumb, dum, dumbur, dumb, dom and dumm.
From ena. Cognate with ene, ene, eine, eina, einen. Equivalent to a.
verändern, equivalent to ändra. Compare forandre.
pretty From grannr, with semantic shift "thin, fine" > "fine, pretty".
From rēþo, probably from rēde, reide. Cognate with rede, reiður.
From svarter, from svartr, from *swartaz, from *swordo-. Cognate with zwart, swart, schwarz, svartur and sort. Related to Latin sordes, sordere.
Originally the past participle form of särskilja.
From hvas, from hvǫss, hvass, from *hwassaz, from *kʷh₁dstós.

ADV.etymology: From tíðligr and tidelik, related to tid.
From even, effen, from evan, from *ebn, from *ebnaz, cognate with even, even. Compare effen. See also jämn.
From nóg, nógr, gnógr, from *ganōgaz. Ultimately from *h₂eh₂nóḱe, perfective of *h₂neḱ-.

From enkil, from enkel, from *ainakulaz, equivalent to en + -el. Compare etymology of simple. Cognate with enkel, enkelt, enkel, ekkil, enkel and 𐌰𐌹𐌽𐌰𐌺𐌻𐍃. Compare also singulus with the same suffix. See also änka and änk
Compound of håg, literally "to one's mind".
pretty From grannr, with semantic shift "thin, fine" > "fine, pretty".

NOUN.etymology: krîch. Unlike and krig, the Swedish word has neutrum gender like kríggj.
From vrāge (Frage), from *frāgu, from *frēgō. Compare frain. Displaced native spörja, spörsmål.
From late Old Swedish stydh, from earlier stuþ, stoþ, from stoð, stuð, from *stuþs, from *stut-. Ultimately from *stew-.
From del, from dēl, deil, from dēl.
Related to ligga and lägga, like German Lage.
From boðskapr. Akin to skap, like Botschaft.
sǫk, from *sakō. Cognate with sak, sak, sag, sök, sake, zaak, Saak, Sook, Sache. An unrelated word that also underwent the transformation in meaning from "legal matter" to "thing, item" is causa.
From stig. Cognate with steg, stig, stig.
From haf, from haf, from *habą, and ultimately from *kap-.
From beskedh, from besched, from bescheden.
From beslut, from beslut, a besluten. 1513. Compare besluit, Beschluss.
From dygn, dyghn, døgn, døghn, from dœgn, from *dōgin-, alternative form of *dōg-. mid-14th century.SO}} Cognate with døgn, døgn and døgn. See also dœgr, døger and dægur.
From hopp, from hōpen, from *hopon, from *hopōn.
From ænginstads, ingenstadz, jngen stedz. Equivalent to stans. Compare ingensteds, ingensteds.
Kanzlei, from cancelli. Cognate of chancellery, chancery.
From Medieval Latin ''correspondens'', cognate with correspondent, Korrespondent. Used in Swedish since 1797.
Borrowed from critique, from criticus, from κριτικός.
From quæld, qvælder, from kveld, from *kwildiz, from *gʷelH-.
From raust, ultimately from a - derivative of *h₃reyH-.922 Related to *runsiz. Cognate with røst.
From skuld, from *skuldiz, from *(s)kéltis. Equivalent to skola.
From spil, from spil and spil, from *spil.
From ūtganger, from útgangr, from *ūtgangaz. Equivalent to gång.
From vir, from *wīʀ (compare vér), from *wīz, from *wéy, plural of *éǵh₂.

PRON.etymology: From vir, from *wīʀ (compare vér), from *wīz, from *wéy, plural of *éǵh₂.

PROPN.etymology: From bilder, from bíldr, from *bīþlaz. An instrumental derivation of *bītaną. Closely related to bila.

VERB.etymology: From gøra, gæra, giora, from gera, gøra, gørva, from *garwijaną. Compare with yare and gear. * The noun is a nominalization of the verb. Literally "doing."
From fār, from fár, from *fawaz.
From praten, probably of sv origin. Compare prate and prattle, práta.
From tro, from trú, from *trūwō.
From vita, from vita, from *witaną, ultimately from *weyd-.
Borrowed from anwenden. Compare anvende.
era
From hiælpa, from hjalpa, from *helpaną, from *ḱelb-, *ḱelp-.
From hoppa, huppa, from hoppa, from *huppōną, from *kewb-.
From halda, halla, holda, holla, from halda, from *haldaną. Further etymology uncertain; possibly from *ḱel-.
From skriva, from skrifa, from *skrībaną, a late borrowing from scrībō. Cognate with skrive, skriva, skrive, skrifa, skriuwe, shrive, schrieven, schrijven and schreiben.
From stælla, from stellen, from stellian, from *stalljan.
From vrāge (Frage), from *frāgu, from *frēgō. Compare frain. Displaced native spörja, spörsmål.
Compare pege and peika.
a

From berætta, from berichten, in correspondence to rätta (verb) and rätt (verb and noun), equivalent to -a or rätta. Cognate with berette, berette, berichten, berechten, bericht, berig, berichten, Bericht and beriichten.
From forsvara, vorantworden (cf. verantworten and verantwoorden), equivalent to svara. Cognate with forsvare, forsvare, forsvara, forsvare.
föra, like German einführen
Verbalization of jobb. Compare jobbe and jobba/jobbe.
From na, from ná. Ultimately from *h₂neḱ-.
From betala, bitala, bittala, from betalen, equivalent to tala.saob Cognate with betale, betale, betala, betale, betalen, betelje, bezahlen, betahlen and betall. Displaced native gälda.
From bevaka. Equivalent to vaka.
era
From fungieren, from fungor,0250.html fungera from *bʰewg-. Cognate with fungere, fungere, fungera, fungere, fungeren, function, fungible, fungovat, fungovať, fungere, fungir, fonctionner.
From verankern or verankeren, equivalent to ankra. Cognate with forankre, forankre and forankra, forankre.
From forklara, from vorklaren,saob equivalent to -a or klara. Cognate with forklare, forklare, forklara, forklare, forklåra, forklåre, verklaren, verklaore, verklaar and erklären.
From neka.
From leda.
From køpa, from keypa, kaupa, from *kaupijaną, *kaupōną, from *kaupô, caupo, caupōnor. Cognate with købe, kjøpe, kaufen, kopen and cheap.
peka
From røra, from hrœra, from *hrōzijaną.
From skapa, from skapa, from *skapjaną. Related to German schaffen and English shape.
From springa, from springa, from *springaną.
From stöd.
From ælska, from ælska (Compare elska). Ultimately from *aliskōną.
